ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_assessment: High quality, text-based PDF with clear legible fields. Contains both ORIGINAL (page 1) and COPY (page 2) versions of the same certificate.
certificate.notes: Using ORIGINAL certificate (page 1). Page 2 is a COPY of the same certificate.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_assessment: High quality, text-based PDF. All fields clearly legible.
invoice.notes: Single-page invoice issued by ARA Paper AB.

lc_check.lc_field_context: The text 'L/C NO.' appears as a separate label/field in box 5 (Remarks/Anmärkningar) of the certificate, followed by the number 592LC2502563. 'L/C NO.' is a standard banking abbreviation for 'Letter of Credit Number' and constitutes a separate field label — not a substring of a reference number.
lc_verification_steps: Step 1: Identified 'L/C NO. 592LC2502563' in certificate box 5 (Remarks).
Step 2: 'L/C NO.' is a recognized bankterm abbreviation (Letter of Credit Number) per section 2.2.
Step 3: It appears as a SEPARATE field label followed by the LC number, not as part of a reference number.
Step 4: The invoice also references 'L/C NO. 592LC2502563' confirming the LC context.
Step 5: LC exception activated per section 2.2. Only country of origin will be verified.

locations_found: Invoice footer: 'ARA Paper AB' (bottom of page 1)

criteria_checked.detail: Certificate consignor 'ARA PAPER AB' is identifiable as the invoice issuer 'ARA Paper AB' in header and footer.
Certificate consignee 'XIAMEN PORT COMMERCE INFORMATION CO.,LTD' matches invoice Customer field exactly.

pairing_validation.motivation: Both consignor and consignee from the certificate are identifiable in the invoice. Document pairing is valid.

comparison_steps.action: Extracted country of origin from certificate box 3: 'SWEDEN'
Searched for explicit origin field in invoice. Found 'Country of origin: Sweden' as a dedicated origin field on invoice page 1.
Compared certificate origin 'SWEDEN' with invoice origin 'SWEDEN' (normalized). Exact match.

rules_applied.description: LC exception — only country of origin is verified.
Identification of origin in invoice via 'Country of origin' field.
Normalization of case (SWEDEN vs Sweden) — identical after normalization.
MATCH — origin country explicitly identified in invoice.

country_of_origin.motivation: Certificate states country of origin as 'SWEDEN' in box 3. Invoice contains a dedicated 'Country of origin' field explicitly stating 'Sweden'. After case normalization, both values are identical. The origin is verified in an explicit origin field as required by section 2.2.
country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 = 'SWEDEN'. Invoice 'Country of origin' field = 'Sweden'. After case normalization: 'SWEDEN' = 'SWEDEN'. Exact match. This is a dedicated origin field, satisfying the LC requirement that origin must be found in an explicit origin field. Confidence 0.99 due to direct explicit match.

rule_evaluation_log.detail: Certificate box 5 contains 'L/C NO. 592LC2502563'. 'L/C NO.' is a recognized bankterm abbreviation appearing as a separate field label in the Remarks section. This is not a substring of a reference number. LC exception activated — only country of origin will be verified. All other checkpoints set to NOT_APPLICABLE.
Document A (Certificate of Origin, pages 1-2: ORIGINAL + COPY) paired with Document B (Invoice, page 1). Using ORIGINAL certificate (page 1). Invoice is in separate file as required.
Consignor 'ARA PAPER AB' identifiable as invoice issuer. Consignee 'XIAMEN PORT COMMERCE INFORMATION CO.,LTD' identifiable as invoice customer. Pairing valid.
Invoice contains explicit 'Country of origin: Sweden' field.
Case normalization: 'SWEDEN' (cert) vs 'Sweden' (invoice) → both 'SWEDEN' after normalization.
Certificate origin 'SWEDEN' explicitly identified in invoice origin field 'Sweden'. MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: LC reference '592LC2502563' might be a regular reference number containing 'LC' as a substring rather than a Letter of Credit
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The certificate uses 'L/C NO.' as a separate field label in box 5 (Remarks), which is a standard banking abbreviation for Letter of Credit Number. The invoice also explicitly states 'Irrevocable Letter of Credit payable 90 days after BL-date' in the Terms of payment field and references 'L/C NO. 592LC2502563' in the commodity summary. This confirms it is a genuine Letter of Credit reference, not a coincidental substring.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet innehåller en hänvisning till Letter of Credit (L/C), vilket innebär att enbart ursprungsland verifieras. Certifikatet anger ursprungsland Sverige och fakturan anger 'Country of origin: Sweden'. Uppgifterna överensstämmer och dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: LC exception activated. Only country of origin verified. Certificate origin 'SWEDEN' matches invoice 'Country of origin: Sweden' exactly. All other checkpoints set to NOT_APPLICABLE per LC exception rule.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

human_readable_report.lc_status: LC exception ACTIVE — L/C NO. 592LC2502563. Only country of origin is verified.

human_readable_report.conclusion: IDENTICAL — LC exception active. Country of origin verified as MATCH. All other checkpoints are NOT_APPLICABLE due to LC exception. Document pair eligible for automatic approval.

output_quality_checks.consistency_check_details: Only one applicable checkpoint (country_of_origin) with status MATCH. comparison_result = IDENTICAL is consistent. No MISMATCH found. No applicable checkpoint with confidence < 0.85.
